specific_mechanism: Roman concrete self-healing via lime-clast / pozzolanic reaction: lime clasts (formed during hot mixing) carry a brittle nanoparticulate architecture; when a microcrack forms, water enters, the lime clast dissolves into a calcium-saturated solution, and it then either recrystallizes as calcium carbonate or reacts with surrounding pozzolanic (volcanic-ash) material to form new calcium-aluminum-silicate-hydrate, sealing the crack from the inside. Repair is stress-triggered (the crack must expose reserves), local (crystals form at the crack), and depletes the local lime-clast reserves over time.
llm_application: An agent runtime that pre-positions dispersed 'lime-clast' reserves (cached alternative responses, fallback heuristics, latent skill modules) throughout its internal state, plus 'pozzolanic' adjuncts (precomputed contextual context, prior-task summaries, refactor templates). Local failures expose these reserves at the failure site, where they 'crystallize' into permanent local refinements. Repair is stress-triggered rather than scheduled, local rather than global, and the reserve population is exhaustible — when reserves at a site are depleted, that site becomes a chronic failure point requiring external intervention.
novelty_claim: VIGIL (arxiv 2512.07094) implements reflective self-healing runtime for LLM agents but uses out-of-band introspective monitoring + structured RBT diagnosis + remediation, not stress-triggered local crystallization from dispersed pre-positioned reserves. 'Self-Healing Software Systems: Lessons from Nature, Powered by AI' (2504.20093) covers nature-inspired self-healing AI in general but doesn't pin a specific mechanism. The Roman concrete framing emphasizes that reserves must be pre-positioned in dispersed form (not centrally accessible), repair must be triggered by failure rather than scheduled, and reserves are exhaustible — three coupled features not jointly captured by existing work.
